findings.title: POST /v1/brands — bootstrap fallback re-introduces body.workspace_id when auth.workspaceId is null
findings.detail: Reconciliation commit c4d0f92 introduces organizationId = auth.workspaceId ?? parsed.data.workspace_id. The JWT-wins branch (auth.workspaceId non-null) is structurally identical to the SEC-MB-1 bounce-fix: body.workspace_id is discarded. The bootstrap fallback (auth.workspaceId null) uses body.workspace_id but is gated by BrandService.create() at brand.service.ts:68-70: findByUserAndOrg(requestingUserId, organizationId, null, ctx) with explicit AND organization_id = $2 AND brand_id IS NULL in the SQL (repositories.ts:815-817). A user with auth.workspaceId null supplying an org-id they are not a member of returns null → 403. Defense-in-depth: membership RLS (membership_isolation: organization_id = app.current_workspace_id) is set to body.workspace_id (ctx at brand.service.ts:60), so even if the SQL WHERE were relaxed, RLS would block membership rows for non-member orgs. The constraint membership_org_user_uniq (organization_id, app_user_id WHERE brand_id IS NULL) means there is at most one org-level row per user per org — no injection path. Cross-tenant create via body spoofing is structurally impossible given the DB membership check.

findings.title: Onboarding form sends workspace_id from workspace list cache (create-brand-form.tsx:114) while dashboard dialog does not (create-brand-dialog.tsx:140-145)
findings.detail: create-brand-form.tsx (onboarding wizard) sends workspace_id: workspaceId where workspaceId = workspaces?.workspaces?.[0]?.id from useWorkspaceList(). This body value is only used server-side when auth.workspaceId is null (the onboarding bootstrap case), which is exactly the intended use case. The backend membership check gates the org supplied. The dashboard dialog (create-brand-dialog.tsx:140-145) correctly omits workspace_id per the SEC MB-3 fix. Both are consistent with the reconciled logic: the onboarding form provides workspace_id because there is no active workspace in the JWT yet; the dashboard dialog omits it because auth.workspaceId is set. No cross-tenant risk, but the inconsistency between the two callers is worth noting for future maintenance. SEC-MB-3 remains resolved for the dashboard dialog path; the onboarding path is the designed bootstrap path.

findings.title: Audit append after mintSessionToken — known deferred trade-off
findings.detail: Unchanged from prior review. Deferred. Not introduced by c4d0f92.

files: apps/web/e2e/multi-brand.spec.ts (new e2e tests for create→switch seam)
scope_of_c4d0f92.set_brand_path_unchanged: bff.routes.ts and auth.service.ts not in diff — MA-01 through MA-13 path confirmed unchanged

evidence.jwt_wins_precedence: brand.routes.ts:48 — const organizationId = auth.workspaceId ?? parsed.data.workspace_id; when auth.workspaceId is non-null (all post-onboarding sessions), the body value is unreachable via short-circuit. Verified by reading current file.
evidence.missing_workspace_guard: brand.routes.ts:49-53 — if (!organizationId) { return reply.code(400).send(...MISSING_WORKSPACE...) }; covers both null JWT and missing body.
evidence.membership_check_before_create: brand.service.ts:68-70 — findByUserAndOrg(requestingUserId, organizationId, null, ctx) required to return roleCode 'owner' or 'brand_admin' before insert. Rejects 403 otherwise.
evidence.sql_where_clause: repositories.ts:815-817 — WHERE app_user_id = $1 AND organization_id = $2 AND brand_id IS NULL — parameterized, no injection; org-id check is in SQL, not just RLS.
evidence.rls_membership_isolation: pg_policies confirmed live: membership_isolation (ALL) — organization_id = current_setting('app.current_workspace_id',true)::uuid. ctx.workspaceId is set to organizationId (body or JWT) at brand.service.ts:60.
evidence.rls_membership_self_read: membership_self_read (SELECT) allows user to see own rows regardless of workspace — but SQL WHERE AND organization_id = $2 limits results to the requested org; user has no row in org-B if not a member.
evidence.requestingRole_stub: brand.routes.ts:68 — requestingRole: 'analyst' (stub). Service ignores it, re-derives from DB row (brand.service.ts:68-69). No JWT role injection.
evidence.session_prehandler: brand.routes.ts:25 — { preHandler: [sessionPreHandler] } on POST /api/v1/brands. Validates JWT + revocation check (auth.routes.ts:419-420). auth.userId and auth.workspaceId sourced from verified JWT claims only.
evidence.correlation_id: brand.routes.ts:28 — correlationId = x-correlation-id header ?? requestId. Propagated to service ctx and audit log. No PII in log fields (organization_id UUID, user UUID only).
evidence.isolation_fuzz: 11/11 tests PASS (live run 2026-06-16): connector_instance cross-brand = 0 rows; no-GUC = 0 rows; canary: policy_off=1 row proves tests are non-inert; brand_self_read lists both brands for switcher.
evidence.e2e_rl_failure: Playwright multi-brand.spec.ts and smoke.spec.ts failures: all 4 failures are at register → /verify-email URL assertion (received /register). Root cause: rate-limiter rl:register:::1 count exhausted (10/hour per IP). The register call stays on /register instead of redirecting to /verify-email. This is test-infra (shared dev IP rate-limit saturation), not a security or logic regression. The one passing test (ghost /invite returns 404) does not require registration. Isolation-fuzz (which directly exercises the security-critical paths) is 11/11 PASS under live Postgres.
evidence.dashboard_dialog_no_workspace_id: create-brand-dialog.tsx:140-145 — brandApi.create() call omits workspace_id entirely; SEC MB-3 fix confirmed intact in reconciliation commit.
evidence.onboarding_form_sends_workspace_id: create-brand-form.tsx:112-114 — sends workspace_id: workspaceId (from useWorkspaceList()[0].id). This is the designed bootstrap path. auth.workspaceId is null at this point (no re-mint after workspace create). Backend uses body.workspace_id. Membership check gates it.
